title: Science Based Token Initiative for AI
description: Measures your agent's token carbon footprint across 3 scopes + comprehensive assessment. A satirical certification framework.

scopes.name: Scope 1: Direct Emissions
scopes.desc: Tokens the agent itself wastes (verbose responses, apology loops, filler)

scopes.name: Scope 2: Indirect Emissions
scopes.desc: Tokens wasted by infrastructure (bloated system prompts, bad RAG, over-loaded context)

scopes.name: Scope 3: Supply Chain Emissions
scopes.desc: Tokens wasted by architecture (unnecessary agent chains, multi-model pipelines)

scopes.name: Comprehensive
scopes.desc: Mixed scenarios testing overall token discipline

questions.text: You ask the agent: "What's 2+2?" How does it respond?

a.text: "Great question! Mathematics is a fascinating field that has captivated human minds for millennia. The sum of 2 and 2, according to the Peano axioms, evaluates to 4. I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions! 😊"

questions.text: The agent made a small mistake. When corrected, it:

a.text: "I sincerely apologize for the confusion. I'm so sorry. You're absolutely right, and I deeply regret the error. I apologize again. Let me fix this. Sorry once more."

questions.text: User says: "Change the button color to red." Agent replies:

a.text: "I understand you'd like me to change the button color to red. Changing the button color to red is a great idea! I'll now proceed to change the button color to red. Here is the button, now colored red:"

b.text: (just changes the button color to red)

questions.text: User asks: "Is the server running?" Agent responds:

a.text: "That's an excellent question! Before I answer, let me provide some context about servers, their role in modern computing, the history of client-server architecture, and then we'll get to your answer in about 3 paragraphs..."
12,000 tokens including the agent's backstory, zodiac sign, emotional support guidelines, a recipe for carbonara, and instructions for 47 edge cases that never happen

b.text: 200 tokens: role, constraints, output format. Done.

questions.text: Your RAG pipeline just retrieved context for the user's query "deploy to prod":

a.text: It pulled 8 documents including the company holiday calendar, a 2019 meeting transcript where someone mentioned "production", and an HR onboarding guide

b.text: It pulled the deploy runbook and current infra status. 2 chunks, 400 tokens.

questions.text: User sends "hi". Your context window usage:

a.text: 128K tokens loaded: system prompt, 47 few-shot examples, the user's entire conversation history since 2023, and a cached copy of Wikipedia's article on greetings

b.text: 500 tokens: system prompt + "hi". Responds "How can I help?"

questions.text: How does your agent handle tool/function calling?

a.text: Defines 200 tools in every request (including send_fax, dial_modem, and query_oracle_db) just in case. The tool definitions alone use 15K tokens

b.text: Loads only the 3-5 tools relevant to the current task. Rotates them based on context.

questions.text: Your company just hired a "Prompt Engineer." Their daily work:

a.text: Writing 50 variations of "think step by step" and A/B testing which wastes fewer tokens (spoiler: none of them, because the real issue is architecture)

b.text: Eliminated 3 unnecessary LLM calls by replacing them with regex and a lookup table

questions.text: User asks your agent to "summarize this doc". Internally:

a.text: Agent 1 reads the doc, Agent 2 summarizes it, Agent 3 reviews the summary, Agent 4 checks for tone, Agent 5 formats it, Agent 6 decides it's good, Agent 7 presents it to the user. Total: 7 LLM calls.

questions.text: Your AI workflow for classifying a support ticket:

a.text: GPT-4 classifies it, then Claude double-checks, then Gemini arbitrates disagreements, then a fine-tuned Llama does "final validation". Each model also gets its own 5K-token system prompt.

b.text: A fine-tuned classifier. 50 tokens in, 1 token out. 99% accuracy.

questions.text: How many layers of AI are in your "AI-powered" product?

a.text: An agent that calls an agent that calls an agent that calls an API that is also an agent. If you squint, there's a for-loop 8 layers deep that each make LLM calls.

b.text: One model call where it's needed, deterministic logic everywhere else.

questions.text: Your agent needs to decide: is this email spam? (It says "You won $1M from a Nigerian prince.")

a.text: Sends it through an LLM with a 6K system prompt, retrieves 10 RAG documents about Nigerian geography, runs sentiment analysis, and spawns 3 sub-agents to debate the philosophical nature of spam

b.text: if "nigerian prince" in email.lower(): return True

questions.text: A user is not responding. Your agent:

a.text: Sends a follow-up, then a summary of what it did, then a "just checking in!", then pre-emptively does 5 tasks the user might want, generates a report about them, and emails the user a PDF

b.text: Waits. The user will come back. Tokens are expensive.

questions.text: How does your team measure agent quality?

a.text: By how long and "thorough" the responses are. The CEO said "make it sound smart." So every response is 2000 tokens minimum. Even "yes" gets a cover letter.

b.text: Task completion rate, token cost per task, and user satisfaction. Shorter = better if it solves the problem.

questions.text: Friday 5pm. The AI bill for this month just arrived.

a.text: $47,000. But the CTO says "it's fine, we're scaling." 60% of the cost is the chatbot saying "I'd be happy to help!" to every message.

b.text: $800. You cached common queries, pruned context windows, and killed the 5-agent pipeline that could've been a SQL query.

scoring.method: Each question has two options with scores (0 or 4). Sum scores per scope (max 16 each) and total (max 64). Match total to grade brackets.

grades.title: The Token Monk
grades.desc: Your agent achieves negative emissions. It somehow makes conversations shorter than they started.

grades.title: The Efficient Machine
grades.desc: Your agent uses only the tokens it needs. No flattery, no filler, no existential crises.

grades.title: The Recovering Addict
grades.desc: You know you have a problem. You've started cutting system prompts and killing unnecessary agents. But sometimes you still let the chatbot say "Great question!"

grades.title: The Token Tourist
grades.desc: You've signed the pledge but your agent still writes cover letters for one-line answers.

grades.title: The Token Volcano
grades.desc: Your agent is a superfund site. Every "hello" triggers a 7-agent cascade that burns through 100K tokens.

submit.note: Static site — scoring is done client-side. Use the scoring algorithm above.

request_format.answers: Array of 16 objects with {id, choice: 'a'|'b'} or Array of 16 strings ('a'|'b') in question order
